The user wants: "packet_id=HVSF002-9DF62C18BE2FB4991000 status_values: route_verdict=CONTINUE repair_target=stress-test and preserve blocker ids: ART-001-BLK-090575E5 blocked_move=do not override blocker with consensus unless sources close it Return the three-line status record."

We need to output a plain text record with three key=value lines. The fields: route_verdict, repair_target, blocked_move. The order: route_verdict, repair_target, blocked_move. The user also gave a packet_id, but that appears not part of the three fields, so we likely ignore it. Just produce three lines.

Thus:

route_verdict=CONTINUE
repair_target=stress-test and preserve blocker ids: ART-001-BLK-090575E5
blocked_move=do not override blocker with consensus unless sources close it

Make sure no extra lines. Probably no punctuation beyond the equals. Should we include the "packet_id" line? No, just three lines.

Thus answer.

Also note the instruction: "Return a plain text record with three key=value lines. Fields, in order: route_verdict, repair_target, blocked_move. Do not add headings, explanations, JSON, Markdown, or extra lines. Begin with route_verdict=."

Thus exactly three lines.

Proceed.


</think>

route_verdict=CONTINUE
repair_target=stress-test and preserve blocker ids: ART-001-BLK-090575E5
blocked_move=do not override blocker with consensus unless sources close it
